test_check_firstAndSecondSecretRevealed_.notice: Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed even         though the first secret is also revealed.

test_check_secondSecretRevealed_fails().notice: Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed.

test_check_secretNotRevealed_fails().notice: Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is not revealed.

test_check_secretRevealedBeforeDelay_fai.notice: Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is revealed but         the delay period has not yet elapsed.

test_check_secretRevealedWithDelayEq_suc.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period has elapsed when the delay         period is non-zero. Here we warp to exactly the delay period.

test_check_secretRevealedWithDelayGt_suc.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period has elapsed when the delay         period is non-zero. Here we warp to after the delay period.

test_check_secretRevealedZeroDelay_succe.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period is zero, meaning the reveal can         happen in the same block as the execution.

test_name_succeeds().notice: Test that the `name` function returns the correct value.

test_reveal_succeeds().notice: Test that basic secret revealing works.

test_reveal_twice_fails().notice: Test that revealing the same secret twice does not work.

test_check_firstAndSecondSecretRevealed_.notice: Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed even         though the first secret is also revealed.

test_check_secondSecretRevealed_fails().notice: Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed.

test_check_secretNotRevealed_fails().notice: Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is not revealed.

test_check_secretRevealedBeforeDelay_fai.notice: Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is revealed but         the delay period has not yet elapsed.

test_check_secretRevealedWithDelayEq_suc.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period has elapsed when the delay         period is non-zero. Here we warp to exactly the delay period.

test_check_secretRevealedWithDelayGt_suc.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period has elapsed when the delay         period is non-zero. Here we warp to after the delay period.

test_check_secretRevealedZeroDelay_succe.notice: Test that the check function returns true when the first secret is revealed but the         second secret is still hidden and the delay period is zero, meaning the reveal can         happen in the same block as the execution.

test_name_succeeds().notice: Test that the `name` function returns the correct value.

test_reveal_succeeds().notice: Test that basic secret revealing works.

test_reveal_twice_fails().notice: Test that revealing the same secret twice does not work.

SecretRevealed(bytes32,bytes).notice: Event emitted when a secret is revealed.

documentation.text: @notice Event emitted when a secret is revealed.
@notice An instance of the CheckSecrets contract.
@notice A secret that must not exist.
@notice A delay period for the check.
@notice Test that the `name` function returns the correct value.
@notice Test that basic secret revealing works.
@notice Test that revealing the same secret twice does not work.

@notice Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is revealed but
         the delay period has not yet elapsed.
@notice Test that the check function returns false when the first secret is not revealed.
@notice Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed.

@notice Test that the check function returns false when the second secret is revealed even
         though the first secret is also revealed.
